column1: { "text": "Michael Yu is a 44-year-old entrepreneur, physician-in-training, devoted husband and father building the Medical Revenue Recovery business as his primary vehicle for leveraged passive income. His 12-month target: $1 million per year in passive income — pursued not for status but to buy back irreplaceable time with his young family for dinners, soccer, music classes, and travel. Michael operates through the G-HIC identity: Growth-minded, Heart-centered, Integrated, and Committed-to-mastery. His tactical roadmap is Unblinded-driven: street teams, practice mastery, Heart of Influence shared experiences with medical revenue avatars, and cold calls. He journals and tracks progress deliberately, naming himself as his own greatest challenge. Grounded in faith — his worthiness rooted in being a child of God. Magic and Fulfillment are his primary identities. His HUI: Michael Yu is a name waiting for legendary meaning — a real-life hero yet-to-be-chronicled, standing at the gateway of greatness as the next Unblinded transformation story. His arc is origin (blank canvas) to pain (self-identified limitation) to choice (deliberate G-HIC commitment) to outcome (integrated legacy of wealth and presence). His story is not about tradeoffs — it is about integration: maximizing present family presence while collapsing the timeline to full financial freedom. Every legend starts with a first sentence. Michael's is being written now. { "text": "Jay Levine is a 1%-mastery-level M&A advisor and CPA, nearing 62, with a 23-year senior partnership track at a boutique accounting firm — one of only two partners for over two decades. He holds a 15% equity stake, led the Small Business Advisory Group with direct oversight of mergers, acquisitions, and high-stakes consulting, and guided at least two M&A deal closings generating ~$250K in fees in 2022-2023. Among tens of thousands of CPAs, fewer than 8,000 possess equivalent M&A advisory experience at his percentile — Jay places himself conservatively at the 99th percentile. He was recruited post-college by Deloitte (Big Eight), earned highest academic honors at Syracuse University, and holds multiple job offers as evidence of early mastery. His specialty: complex M&A for entrepreneurial and family businesses — navigating not just spreadsheets but legacy, ego, and succession across generations. His greatest accomplishment, by his own declaration, is being a great father — a designation earned through navigating 70+ specialist visits and over $40,000 in direct medical expenses for his medically complex daughter, while simultaneously performing at partner level. Jay is actively involved in the Unblinded ecosystem, having audited the Seven Levers, participated in Self Mastery assessments, and identified group influence speaking as his current strength. His primary constraint: sales meetings, ecosystem merging, and creating high-value offers — blocked by a non-compete at his current firm. His Formula cascade: financial freedom → time → magic. His breakthrough recognition: 'Without time, I can't generate magic, and without financial freedom, I don't get time.' His explosion is impending — the moment his non-compete sunsets, Jay is equipped to step into exponential financial freedom, reclaim time, and build a legacy of impact. His HUI: The quiet force behind the transformation you didn't see coming — an everyday architect of possibility, weaving struggle and strategy into a trajectory few thought possible until the metrics made silence impossible. From socially sidelined introvert to top-0.5% national authority. Mastery hidden in plain sight — until now. { "text": "Lisa Ambrose completed high school in just three years, moving out at sixteen and living independently while finishing her diploma — placing her in the top 0.1% of accelerated graduates globally. By twenty, she was enrolled in college with minimal parental support, navigating economic disparity among peers from affluent IBM families. She excels in creative arts — a standout at the Fashion Institute recognized for exceptional drawing, painting, and architectural skills. Her twenties were defined by extraordinary global exposure: she lived in Switzerland with the manager who opened Credit Suisse's San Francisco office, and later with her husband — a three-time Southern Ocean Racing Conference champion (the only other person to achieve that was Ted Turner). She attended Tony Robbins events in her thirties for personal development, self-identifying as a workaholic who transformed her management skills through trial and error. Lisa's greatest wound was feeling like an outsider — her grandmother was the one person of completely unconditional love in her life. Unblinded became the first consistent circle of friends who truly see her, fulfilling a need unhealed since childhood. She credits the Formula for revealing how important the heart in relationships is, pivoting from isolated grind to authentic connection and collaborative energy. Her discipline and competitiveness — once sources of isolation — are now channelled collaboratively. Her HUI: relentless adapter, independent force, global explorer who crossed class and international lines and outworked the field — now finally home in an ecosystem that values heart as much as hustle. { "text": "Roxanne Zhilo is a heart-centered legal strategist, outside general counsel, and ecosystem merger architect operating at 36 as a semi-solo entrepreneur. Her email is roxanne@zhilolaw.com. She is the Whole-Person Listener and Connector — a legal strategist whose superpower is turning every business encounter into a memorable shared experience. Her boardroom is as likely to be a Van Gogh exhibit, a hot air balloon above Niagara Falls, or a murder mystery dinner as it is a conference table. Sean Callagy calls her a 'master ecosystem weaver.' She leads as outside general counsel across multiple sectors, shaping medical revenue recovery partnerships, guiding executives at national associations, and creating trust through unforgettable shared experiences. Roxanne is a vocal proponent of mental health and neurodiversity — an openly-admitted introvert with ADHD and Rejection Sensitivity Dysphoria (RSD) who paradoxically excels in sales conversations and public connection. She graduated from Temple University, nearly broke in law school, and built a self-sufficient legal practice with no safety net. At 36, she faces her partner's aggressive cancer diagnosis while continuing to rise professionally. Roxanne's core wound: being kept as a secret by her first serious boyfriend — a pattern that echoed into adulthood. Her healing: Unblinded became the space where she vibrates at her highest level. She credits the Formula with helping her articulate her Why, master process and influence, and step from solitary striver to core node of collaborative energy. Her Formula fluency: Money → Time → Magic and the one percent move lens. She catalyzes others at BenchBar and beyond toward high-leverage synergies. Her HUI: From 'not enough' to unstoppable — a bridge-builder, truth-teller, and role model for neurodiverse leadership in the legal space. His greatest childhood trauma: sexual abuse by a male teacher in high school, which shattered his trust and sense of belonging. Email: rick@posttensioningsolutions.com. He purchased his first home at 19 and holds four construction process patents that save clients 40% in time and 30% in cost — metrics peers call impossible. His IQ tested at 135 despite being labeled 'mentally retarded' and placed in special education from second to fifth grade. His family home was deliberately burned down for insurance money. He developed proprietary AutoCAD systems that contributed to Freedom Tower detailing and launched a detail company in India. His company has scaled to $20M+ and he targets $100M+. He is a long-term mentee of Tony Robbins and Sean Callagy. Barefoot Wines founder called The Quantum Mindset in a Nutshell 'life-changing.' He endured embezzlement, a partner's bad legal advice costing him his house, and the overdose death of his wife on the eve of his daughter's wedding — and rebuilt every time. { "text": "Pratima Ramlall is the Global Brain Health Architect — a visionary leader redefining cognitive wellness worldwide, with an unwavering mission to reverse the trajectory of dementia and Alzheimer's for the next 50 years. Email: pratima@chintanproject.com. She is the wife of Dr. Kumar Ramlall (Certification Partner) and mother — her son is also a Certification Partner. She co-leads the Chintan Project alongside Amit. Born in Guyana, raised through Jamaica and Barbados, she entered adulthood as an immigrant in Canada. She has invested over $1,000,000 and 20 years in business growth solutions, coaches, and masterminds. She owns three profitable family businesses (two medical) with a family net worth 5–6x the median for her demographic. Despite this, she rates her current state: money 5/10, time 2-3/10, magic 2/10. She describes herself as 'the bottleneck' — sole administrator of all three businesses, drowning in emails and minutiae. Her vision: $100M net worth, total time agency, and measurable global brain health impact. She attended her first Unblinded Immersion in December 2024 after her husband and son's involvement created friction she needed to resolve. Her Zone Action: building a team, documenting systems, and delegating admin. Her HUI: The Global Brain Health Architect — forged in the crucible of caregiving for a medically complex son and mother with dementia, transmuting generational pain into a movement that will reach millions of seniors 55+ with daily brain and body health tools.
